title: DENTAL PROGRAM - 211info
text: HIV ALLIANCE DENTAL PROGRAM DENTAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dental care to people living with HIV. Clinic services include preventative care, restorative care, dentures, and bridge work. For clients that need to travel to access services, transportation and travel lodging may be available. 2460 Willamette Street Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: People living with HIV who are enrolled in HIV Alliance Care Coordination services Hours: Call for information Email (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 342-5088 Toll-free (866) 470-3419 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Must be referred by HIV Alliance. Contact Dental Case Manager or HIV Case Manager for more information. FEES: None for individuals with income below 300% Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Some charges may apply depending on the treatment needed and the individual's income level. DOCUMENTS: Completed referral forms LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Baker, Benton, Clatsop, Coos, Crook, Curry, Deschutes, Douglas, Gilliam, Grant, Harney, Jackson, Jefferson, Josephine, Klamath, Lake, Lane, Lincoln, Linn, Malheur, Marion, Morrow, Polk, Sherman, Umatilla, Wallowa, Wasco, and Wheeler Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Cavity Prevention Measures * AIDS/HIV Dental Hygiene * AIDS/HIV Dental Prosthodontics * AIDS/HIV Endodontics * AIDS/HIV Oral/Maxillofacial Surgery * AIDS/HIV Periodontics * AIDS/HIV If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
